prompt: Fact: Plants need sunlight to make food. Question: A plant placed in a dark closet for many weeks will most likely A) grow taller B) bloom flowers C) wilt and die D) change color to green
Fact: Friction creates heat between two surfaces. Question: Rubbing your hands together quickly will A) cool them down B) make them feel warmer C) make them numb D) have no effect
Fact: Magnets attract objects containing iron. Question: Which of these will a magnet pick up? A) A plastic spoon B) A glass cup C) A steel paperclip D) A wooden block

Fact: Liquids take the shape of their container. Question: When water is poured from a tall glass into a wide bowl, the water A) keeps its tall shape B) becomes a solid C) takes the shape of the bowl D) evaporates instantly

Fact: Animals adapt to their environment to survive. Question: A polar bear has thick white fur because it A) lives in a sandy desert B) lives in a cold snowy place C) eats only plants D) sleeps underwater
Fact: The water cycle moves water between Earth and the atmosphere. Question: Water on the ground turning into vapor and rising is called A) precipitation B) condensation C) evaporation D) freezing

Fact: Sound travels in waves through air. Question: An astronaut on the Moon cannot hear another astronaut speaking without a radio because A) sound travels too fast B) there is no air to carry sound C) the Moon absorbs sound D) gravity blocks sound

Fact: A food chain shows how energy moves between living things. Question: In a meadow with grass, rabbits, and foxes, the energy flow is A) fox to rabbit to grass B) grass to fox to rabbit C) grass to rabbit to fox D) rabbit to grass to fox
Fact: The Sun is the main source of energy for life on Earth. Question: Without sunlight, plants would A) grow faster B) be unable to make food C) live longer D) change color to purple

Fact: Heat moves from warmer objects to cooler objects. Question: An ice cube placed in a warm drink will A) make the drink colder and the ice will melt B) keep the drink hot C) freeze the drink D) stay frozen forever
Fact: Earth completes one rotation on its axis every 24 hours. Question: This rotation causes A) the seasons B) day and night C) tides D) earthquakes
Fact: Mass is the amount of matter in an object. Question: Which of these has the greatest mass? A) A feather B) A pencil C) A brick D) A piece of paper

Fact: Plants release oxygen during photosynthesis. Question: A room with many growing plants will most likely have A) less oxygen B) more oxygen during daylight C) the same amount of oxygen D) no oxygen at all

Fact: Erosion is the wearing away of rock or soil by wind or water. Question: A river that flows over rocks for many years will A) make the rocks larger B) smooth and wear down the rocks C) freeze the rocks D) make the rocks heavier

Fact: Gravity pulls objects toward the center of the Earth. Question: When you drop a ball, it falls because A) the wind pushes it down B) gravity pulls it down C) the ball is heavy D) air sucks it down
Fact: Conductors let electricity flow through them easily. Question: Which is the best conductor of electricity? A) Glass B) Wood C) Copper wire D) Plastic

Fact: All living things are made of cells. Question: A drop of pond water examined under a microscope is most likely to show A) only minerals B) tiny living organisms C) crystals only D) salt grains only
Fact: A solid can melt to a liquid when heated. Question: A block of butter left on a hot stove will A) freeze solid B) become a gas instantly C) melt into a liquid D) remain unchanged
Fact: Animals breathe in oxygen and breathe out carbon dioxide. Question: Which gas do humans need to breathe in to live? A) Carbon dioxide B) Hydrogen C) Oxygen D) Helium

Fact: The Moon reflects light from the Sun. Question: We can see the Moon at night because A) the Moon makes its own light B) the Moon reflects sunlight C) stars light the Moon D) Earth lights the Moon

Fact: A seed needs water, warmth, and air to germinate. Question: A seed kept dry in a sealed bag in a cool dark cupboard will A) grow into a plant B) not germinate C) flower quickly D) photosynthesize

Fact: Some animals hibernate in winter to save energy. Question: Bears sleep deeply through winter mainly because A) they enjoy long naps B) food is scarce and it conserves energy C) the cold makes them happy D) other animals attack in summer
Fact: The Earth's tilt causes the seasons. Question: When the Northern Hemisphere tilts toward the Sun, it is A) winter there B) spring there C) summer there D) autumn there
Fact: Pollination helps plants reproduce. Question: Bees help flowers reproduce by A) eating the flower B) moving pollen between flowers C) digging up roots D) breaking the stems
Fact: Forces can change the speed or direction of an object. Question: Pushing a stationary toy car will A) keep it still B) make it spin only C) cause it to move D) make it disappear

Fact: Mixing iron with oxygen and water forms rust. Question: A bicycle left out in the rain for months may A) become magnetic B) develop rust C) turn into copper D) grow heavier from absorbed water only
Fact: A telescope is used to view distant objects in space. Question: A scientist wanting to look closely at Saturn would best use A) a microscope B) a periscope C) a telescope D) binoculars at noon

Fact: Decomposers break down dead material and return nutrients to the soil. Question: Mushrooms and bacteria help an ecosystem mainly by A) eating live animals B) producing sunlight C) recycling nutrients D) cooling the air
Fact: A complete circuit allows electric current to flow. Question: A flashlight will not light if A) the battery is fresh B) the wires connect properly C) the circuit is broken D) the switch is on
Fact: The states of matter include solid, liquid, gas, and plasma. Question: Water vapor is an example of which state of matter? A) Solid B) Liquid C) Gas D) Plasma
